name: ONA
description: Analog voltage-controlled oscillator. CEM/AS 3340 triangle core with eight simultaneous outputs: sine, triangle, saw, pulse, two PWM-mixed complex waves, and two square sub-octaves.

controls.name: OCT
controls.detail: Positions 0–7 · each step adds one octave

controls.name: FINE
controls.description: Fine-tune around the OCT setting. Active in both VCO and LFO modes.

controls.name: FM Mode
controls.description: Selects response curve of the FM input.

controls.name: Mode
controls.description: Swaps core between audio and sub-audio ranges.
controls.detail: VCO: ~20Hz–40kHz+ · LFO: ~60s per cycle up to low audio

controls.name: FM
controls.description: Attenuator for the FM input. No signal below unity CCW.

controls.name: PW
controls.description: Sets pulse-width base. Acts as attenuator for PW input when a cable is patched.

inputs.name: FM
inputs.description: Frequency modulation. Behaviour (linear vs exponential) set by FM Mode switch; depth set by FM knob.

inputs.name: OCT
inputs.description: Primary 1V/oct pitch input. Sums with OCT switch and FINE knob.

inputs.name: SYNC
inputs.description: Hard sync. Rising edge resets the triangle core; affects every output jack simultaneously.

inputs.name: PW
inputs.description: Pulse-width CV. Modulates duty cycle of the Pulse output and the PWM pulse inside Complex 1 and Complex 2.

outputs.name: Sine
outputs.description: Smooth sine waveform. Lowest harmonic content.

outputs.name: Triangle
outputs.description: Triangle waveform derived directly from the core.

outputs.name: Saw
outputs.description: Rising sawtooth. Harmonically richest of the classic outputs.

outputs.name: Pulse
outputs.description: Variable-width pulse. Duty cycle set by PW knob and PW input.

outputs.name: Complex 1
outputs.description: Triangle mixed with the PWM pulse. Unique shifting timbre that responds to PW.

outputs.name: Complex 2
outputs.description: Saw mixed with the PWM pulse. Buzzier sibling of Complex 1.

outputs.name: Sub -1
outputs.description: Square waveform one octave below the main tuning.

outputs.name: Sub -2
outputs.description: Square waveform two octaves below the main tuning.

behaviors.name: VCO / LFO mode switch
behaviors.description: Flips the core between audio-rate VCO and sub-audio LFO ranges. LFO range reaches roughly one minute per cycle. All eight outputs remain active in LFO mode — useful for shaped phased modulation sources.

behaviors.name: Linear vs exponential FM
behaviors.description: EXP is the standard musical FM path (octaves per volt). LIN is DC-coupled around the core frequency; patch audio-rate for classic linear-FM bells and metallic tones, CV for vibrato that tracks depth rather than pitch.

behaviors.name: PW knob as attenuator
behaviors.description: Unpatched, PW knob sets a static duty cycle from 1% to 100%. Patching a cable into PW turns the knob into the CV attenuator, and the duty cycle is driven entirely by the 0–5V input.

behaviors.name: PW affects Complex outs
behaviors.description: The Complex 1 and Complex 2 outputs mix the core's triangle or saw with the PWM pulse, so PW modulation reshapes their timbre rather than just the Pulse output. Slow LFO into PW gives evolving harmonic sweeps on the Complex jacks.

behaviors.name: Hard sync
behaviors.description: Resets the triangle core. Patch a second VCO's pitched output here and sweep ONA's tuning for classic sync-lead tones across all output jacks simultaneously.

behaviors.name: Multiple voices from one module
behaviors.trigger: Patching two outputs to separate paths
behaviors.description: With eight simultaneous outputs from a single core, ONA can drive two or more parallel voices at the same pitch — e.g. Sine into a sub mixer and Saw into a filter voice, with Sub -1 reinforcing the fundamental.

behaviors.name: Calibration trimmers
behaviors.description: Three trimmers behind the panel: Scale (1V/oct tracking), Base Freq (C anchor with FINE at noon), and Oct Switch (linearity across the 8-position switch). Factory calibrated; re-tune after 5–10 minute warm-up if drift appears.

behaviors.name: Skiff-friendly depth
behaviors.description: 20mm depth with the ribbon attached fits shallow cases. Power connector keyed with the red stripe marked on the PCB — reversed ribbons damage the circuit.

patchIdeas.name: Sub-reinforced bass
patchIdeas.patch: VCO mode · Saw into filter · Sub -1 mixed under the Saw · pitch CV from sequencer · layered sub octave fattens the low end without detuning voices.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ONA:
* Mode: VCO
* OCT: 2
* PW: noon

Mixer:

Filter:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> ONA (OCT)
- ONA (Saw) -> Filter (In)
- ONA (Sub -1) -> Mixer (In 2)
- Filter (Out) -> Mixer (In 1)

patchIdeas.name: Evolving complex pad
patchIdeas.patch: VCO mode · Complex 1 into low-pass filter · slow LFO into PW input · duty cycle modulation reshapes the triangle-plus-PWM mix for slow harmonic drift.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ONA:
* Mode: VCO
* FM Mode: EXP
* PW: noon

LFO:

Filter:

- LFO (Out) >> ONA (PW)
- ONA (Complex 1) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Linear FM bells
patchIdeas.patch: VCO mode · FM Mode LIN · second VCO's sine into FM input · FM knob near unity · carrier Sine out · inharmonic metallic FM tones that keep pitch stable.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ONA:
* Mode: VCO
* FM Mode: LIN
* FM: ~3 o'clock

VCO 2:

VCA:

- VCO 2 (Sine) -> ONA (FM)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> ONA (OCT)
- ONA (Sine) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Sync lead
patchIdeas.patch: Two VCOs · master VCO's square into ONA SYNC · sweep ONA OCT/FINE or modulate ONA pitch with an envelope · Saw out for classic hard-sync formant sweep.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ONA:
* Mode: VCO
* FM Mode: EXP

VCO 2:

Envelope:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- VCO 2 (Square) -> ONA (SYNC)
- Envelope (Out) >> ONA (FM)
- ONA (Saw) -> Filter (In)

patchIdeas.name: LFO with phased outputs
patchIdeas.patch: LFO mode · use Sine as smooth LFO, Triangle for a slightly harder shape, Pulse for gate division, Sub -1 for half-rate gate — one slow core drives four modulation lines.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ONA:
* Mode: LFO
* OCT: 0
* PW: noon

Filter:

VCA:

Envelope:

Drum:

- ONA (Sine) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- ONA (Triangle) >> VCA (CV)
- ONA (Pulse) g> Envelope (Gate)
- ONA (Sub -1) g> Drum (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: PWM stack
patchIdeas.patch: VCO mode · slow triangle LFO into PW · Complex 2 into filter · audio-rate shimmer as duty cycle sweeps reshape the saw+pulse mix — fatter than classic PWM square alone.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ONA:
* Mode: VCO
* PW: ~3 o'clock

LFO:
* Shape: Triangle

Filter:

VCA:

Envelope:

- LFO (Out) >> ONA (PW)
- ONA (Complex 2) -> Filter (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual-voice unison
patchIdeas.patch: VCO mode · Saw and Complex 1 into separate filters · one filter fast envelope, the other slow · stereo mix — a single oscillator spawning two timbrally distinct voices at identical pitch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ONA:
* Mode: VCO
* OCT: 3

Filter L:

Filter R:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> ONA (OCT)
- ONA (Saw) -> Filter L (In)
- ONA (Complex 1) -> Filter R (In)
- Filter L (Out) -> Mixer (L)
- Filter R (Out) -> Mixer (R)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate sync rhythms
patchIdeas.patch: LFO mode · clock into SYNC · sub and pulse outs drive drum triggers at synced sub-divisions · the resets produce polyrhythms that lock to clock.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ONA:
* Mode: LFO
* OCT: 2

Clock:

- Clock (Out) c> ONA (SYNC)
- ONA (Pulse) t> Drum 1 (Trig)
- ONA (Sub -2) t> Drum 2 (Trig)
